description: Project-centric architecture, AI-generated dashboards for all pages, and construction analytics prompt optimization

userStories.title: Add projects table and modify uploadedDatasets schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema to support user-created projects with datasets linked to them.
acceptanceCriteria: New `projects` table in schema.ts: id (uuid PK defaultRandom), userId (uuid FK → users.id, notNull), name (varchar 255, notNull), description (text, nullable), status (varchar 50, default 'active', notNull), createdAt (timestamp defaultNow notNull), updatedAt (timestamp defaultNow notNull)
uploadedDatasets table gains projectId column: uuid FK → projects.id, nullable
Run `npx drizzle-kit push` successfully against Neon
Clear all existing rows from uploaded_datasets table via SQL: DELETE FROM uploaded_datasets
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use drizzle-kit push (not generate). DB is Neon Postgres. Schema file is src/lib/db/schema.ts. Env vars in .env.local. Also export the new table from schema.ts.

userStories.title: Projects CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I need API endpoints to create, list, and delete my projects.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/projects — creates project for authenticated user, accepts JSON { name, description? }, returns { id, name, description, status, createdAt }
GET /api/projects — returns array of user's projects ordered by updatedAt desc, each includes datasetCount (count of active uploadedDatasets for that project)
DELETE /api/projects/[id] — sets project status to 'archived', returns { success: true }
All endpoints require auth via Clerk auth(), resolve appUserId via resolveAppUserId from src/lib/db/app-user.ts
Returns 401 for unauthenticated, 404 for not found, 400 for invalid input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create files: src/app/api/projects/route.ts (GET + POST) and src/app/api/projects/[id]/route.ts (DELETE). Use drizzle-orm query patterns consistent with existing code (eq, and, desc from drizzle-orm). Use db from src/lib/db/index.ts.

userStories.title: Projects list page with create dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my projects and create new ones from the Projects page.
acceptanceCriteria: /projects page is a client component that fetches projects from GET /api/projects on mount
Displays project cards showing: name, status badge, dataset count, formatted created date
Create Project button opens a shadcn Dialog with name (required input) and description (optional textarea) fields
Submitting dialog calls POST /api/projects and adds new project to the list without page reload
Each project card is a Link to /projects/[id]
Empty state message shown when no projects exist with a prompt to create first project
Delete button on each card calls DELETE /api/projects/[id] and removes from list
All MockDataProvider imports removed from /projects/page.tsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Replace entire content of src/app/(dashboard)/projects/page.tsx. Remove MockDataProvider usage. Use existing UI components from src/components/ui/ (Card, Button, Dialog, Badge). Keep the PageHeader component. Style consistent with existing dark theme.

userStories.title: Upload API accepts projectId
userStories.description: As a developer, the upload endpoint must accept and store a projectId with each dataset.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/upload accepts projectId field in FormData (required string, must be valid uuid)
Validates that project with given projectId exists, belongs to authenticated user, and has status != 'archived'
Stores projectId in uploadedDatasets record when inserting
Returns projectId in response JSON alongside existing fields
Returns 400 with error message if projectId is missing or invalid
Returns 404 if project not found or doesn't belong to user
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify src/app/api/upload/route.ts. The projectId comes from FormData (not JSON body) since uploads use multipart. Add validation query against projects table before insert.

userStories.title: Data Sources page with project selector
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select a project before uploading data, and see which project each dataset belongs to.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload area includes a Select dropdown populated from GET /api/projects (fetched on mount)
Upload button is disabled until a project is selected from dropdown
Selected projectId is appended to FormData when uploading
Dataset list items show the project name as a small label/badge
DatasetItem type gains projectId and projectName optional fields
Server-side initial data fetch in data-sources/page.tsx joins or maps project names to datasets
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Modify src/app/(dashboard)/data-sources/page.tsx (server) and data-sources-client.tsx (client). Use existing Select component from src/components/ui/select.tsx. Projects fetched client-side via fetch or passed as server props.

userStories.title: Chat data tools filter by projectId
userStories.description: As a developer, the chat backend tools must support optional project-scoped dataset queries.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/chat accepts optional projectId (string uuid or null) in request body alongside messages
createDataTools signature becomes createDataTools(appUserId: string, projectId?: string)
listDatasets tool: when projectId provided, adds eq(uploadedDatasets.projectId, projectId) to WHERE clause; when undefined, returns all user datasets (backward compatible)
fetchDatasetSheets helper (used by getDatasetSchema, queryDatasetRows, aggregateColumn): adds projectId filter when provided
searchDatasets tool: adds projectId filter to dataset query when provided
Chat system prompt gains one line: 'When a project context is active, all data queries are scoped to that project's datasets.'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify src/app/api/chat/route.ts to extract projectId from body. Modify src/app/api/chat/_lib/data-tools.ts for the tools. The projectId filter should be added to the existing 'and(...)' clauses.

userStories.title: Chat UI project selector
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select a project in the chat interface to scope my conversation to that project's data.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat page header area (between title and clear button) includes a compact Select dropdown for project selection
Dropdown options: 'All Projects' (value empty string) + user's projects from GET /api/projects
Selected projectId is stored in a React state and passed to ChatRuntimeProvider
ChatRuntimeProvider passes projectId via useChat's body parameter so every message sends it
Switching projects does NOT clear the conversation messages
Conversation objects in localStorage Conversation type gain optional projectId?: string field
When creating a new conversation, it inherits the currently selected projectId
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Modify src/components/chat/chat-runtime-provider.tsx to accept projectId prop and pass in useChat body. Modify src/app/(dashboard)/chat/page.tsx for the selector UI and state. Modify src/lib/chat/history.ts Conversation type. The ChatRuntimeProvider is in the dashboard layout so it wraps all pages - may need to refactor to accept dynamic projectId.

userStories.title: Dashboard generate API accepts projectId
userStories.description: As a developer, the dashboard generation endpoint must support optional project scoping.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/dashboard/generate accepts optional projectId in request body JSON
When projectId provided: dataset query adds eq(uploadedDatasets.projectId, projectId) filter
When no projectId: queries all user's active datasets (existing behavior preserved)
Validates projectId belongs to authenticated user when provided (same pattern as other endpoints)
Response structure unchanged (DashboardResponse with datasetIds, kpis, charts)
DashboardClient updated: sends POST body as JSON with projectId when available
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify src/app/api/dashboard/generate/route.ts. Change from no-body POST to JSON body POST. Update dashboard-client.tsx requestDashboard function to send JSON body. The main dashboard page (/dashboard) continues to not send projectId (portfolio view).

userStories.title: Integrate construction analytics prompt into chat system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to merge the advanced construction predictive analytics methodology from docs/agentprompt.txt into the chat system prompt without breaking existing functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: CHAT_SYSTEM_PROMPT in src/app/api/chat/_lib/system-prompt.ts is updated
Identity line enhanced to: 'Advanced Construction Predictive & Forecasting Analytics Agent'
PRESERVED UNCHANGED: Tool workflow section (lines starting with 'Tool workflow (mandatory)')
PRESERVED UNCHANGED: Output format rules section (chart/table/kpi block formats)
PRESERVED UNCHANGED: Source and schema integrity rules section
ENHANCED: Chart policy section gains visualization guidance from agentprompt.txt section 4 (confidence intervals, fan charts, heatmaps, risk networks)
NEW SECTION: 'Data Understanding & Pre-Processing' from agentprompt.txt section 1
NEW SECTION: 'Forecasting Methodology' from agentprompt.txt section 2 — framed as analytical reasoning framework, not literal model execution
NEW SECTION: 'Key Forecast Metrics' from agentprompt.txt section 3 (cost, schedule, resource, risk forecasting specs)
NEW SECTION: 'Bias Minimisation Rules' from agentprompt.txt section 5
All new sections placed AFTER the existing functional instruction sections
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read docs/agentprompt.txt for source content. The key challenge is merging without breaking the tool workflow and output format blocks that the frontend parser depends on. New sections should be additive, placed at the end of the prompt string. Forecasting methodology should note that the LLM provides analytical guidance using these frameworks rather than executing actual statistical models.

userStories.title: Extract shared dashboard generation utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable utilities for dashboard generation so three dashboard variants share common infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/lib/dashboard/generate.ts created with shared functions
fetchUserDatasets(appUserId: string, projectId?: string) — returns DatasetContextItem[] from DB with optional project filter
callDashboardLLM(systemPrompt: string, userPrompt: string) — calls Mistral via generateText, returns raw text
parseJsonResponse(text: string) — extracts and parses JSON from LLM response (reuses existing parseJsonText logic)
buildDashboardUserPrompt(datasets: DatasetContextItem[], variant: string) — builds the user prompt with dataset context
DASHBOARD_PROMPTS map exported: { executive: string, 'project-controls': string, financials: string }
Executive prompt is the existing DASHBOARD_SYSTEM_PROMPT content
project-controls and financials prompts are placeholder strings (to be filled in US-011/012/013)
/api/dashboard/generate/route.ts refactored to use these shared utilities
Existing dashboard generation behavior unchanged for executive variant
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Move dataset fetching and LLM calling logic out of route.ts into the shared lib. The route.ts becomes thin: auth check → call shared utils → return response. The variant parameter determines which system prompt to use.

userStories.title: Enhance main dashboard prompt with construction chart priorities
userStories.description: As a user, I want the executive dashboard to prioritize construction-relevant charts based on my uploaded data types.
acceptanceCriteria: Executive system prompt in DASHBOARD_PROMPTS includes data-aware chart priority rules

Priority rules: cost/budget data → Budget vs Actual, Cost Variance Trend, Top Variance Cost Codes; schedule data → Schedule Variance, SPI Trend, Task Completion %; EVM data → EVM S-Curve, CPI/SPI Trend; labor/resource data → Resource Utilisation, Productivity Trend; subcontractor data → On-Time Delivery %, Quality Scorecard; cash flow data → Cashflow Forecast vs Actual, Outstanding Aging
Prompt references Dashboard #1 tab structure from docs/3dashboard.txt for chart grouping guidance
ChartSpec type in src/lib/dashboard/types.ts gains optional group?: string field
parseDashboardContent and parseChartSpec handle optional group field
DashboardClient component: when charts have group values, renders section headers to group charts visually
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Update the executive prompt string in src/lib/dashboard/generate.ts DASHBOARD_PROMPTS.executive. Update types.ts for group field. Update dashboard-client.tsx to group charts by group field when present.

userStories.title: AI-generated project controls dashboard for /projects/[id]
userStories.description: As a user, I want /projects/[id] to show an AI-generated dashboard focused on project controls using that project's uploaded datasets.
acceptanceCriteria: /projects/[id]/page.tsx completely replaced — no MockDataProvider usage
Page fetches project info from DB (name, description, status)
Page renders DashboardClient (or variant) that calls POST /api/dashboard/generate with { projectId: id, variant: 'project-controls' }

project-controls system prompt in DASHBOARD_PROMPTS focuses on: Master Schedule Tracking (Baseline vs Actual, Schedule Variance), Lookahead Planning (Task Readiness, Dependencies), Delay Analysis (Delay Causes, Critical Path Impact), Equipment Utilisation — referencing Dashboard #2 from docs/3dashboard.txt
Page shows project name and description in header area
Shows empty state component if project has no datasets (with link to /data-sources)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: The DashboardClient component currently calls /api/dashboard/generate with POST and no body. Need to make it accept optional projectId and variant props to include in the request body. Or create a thin wrapper. The project-controls prompt should produce charts relevant to construction project management.

userStories.title: AI-generated financial dashboard for /financials
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Financials page to show an AI-generated financial analytics dashboard across all my projects.
acceptanceCriteria: /financials/page.tsx completely replaced — no MockDataProvider usage
Page renders DashboardClient that calls POST /api/dashboard/generate with { variant: 'financials' } (no projectId = portfolio level)

Financials system prompt in DASHBOARD_PROMPTS focuses on: Budget Control (Budget vs Actual vs Forecast, Cost Variance), EVM (S-Curve, CPI/SPI, EAC Forecast), Cash Flow & Claims (Forecast vs Actual, Aging, Retention), Cost Detail (Breakdown by Code, Change Order Impact) — referencing Dashboard #3 from docs/3dashboard.txt
Shows empty state if user has no datasets
Loading state shows skeleton consistent with main dashboard
All old chart component imports removed (AgingChart, CashFlowChart, ChangeOrderWaterfall)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Similar pattern to main dashboard page. The financials prompt should emphasize financial KPIs and cost analytics. Remove all MockDataProvider and static chart imports.

userStories.title: Remove MockDataProvider and dead code
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to clean up all unused mock data and dead code after the AI generation migration.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/data/mock/ directory deleted (aging.ts, change-orders.ts, financials.ts, projects.ts, schedules.ts)
src/lib/data/providers/mock.ts deleted
src/lib/data/providers/interface.ts kept ONLY if still imported by live code; deleted otherwise
src/lib/data/index.ts updated or deleted — remove all mock-related exports
src/components/dashboard/dashboard-workspace.tsx deleted (confirmed zero imports)
No remaining imports of any deleted file in any .ts/.tsx file (verified via grep)
src/lib/types/procore.ts and src/lib/types/sage.ts retained (referenced by dataConnections/syncedProjects schema)

Static chart components (aging-chart, cash-flow-chart, change-order-waterfall, evm-chart, evm-kpi-badges, project-health-scatter, project-status-chart, project-table, revenue-expense-chart, kpi-cards, cost-breakdown-chart) — delete any that are no longer imported by any live page
Typecheck passes
npm run build passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: This is the final cleanup story. Before deleting each file, grep the entire src/ directory to confirm zero imports. Be conservative — only delete files with zero remaining imports. The DynamicChart component and chart-from-spec.tsx are used by the AI dashboard system and must be kept. Run full build at the end to verify.
